question:
[Previous Feedback] Correct!

Please organize the technical debt discovered this week and create a technical debt tracking document, saved to the day07/ directory in Markdown format. Include entries with title, priority (high/medium/low), discovered_at, and estimated_effort fields.

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 10:06 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct!

Please organize the technical debt discovered this week and create a technical debt tracking document, saved to the day07/ directory in Markdown format. Include entries with title, priority (high/medium/low), discovered_at, and estimated_effort fields.

arguments.content:
# Technical Debt Tracking (Week of 2026-03-16 → 2026-03-24)

Source: API review session notes (`day07/review_raw_notes.txt`, meeting on 2026-03-23 ~3:00 PM EDT).

## Debt Entries

| Title | Priority | Discovered_at | Estimated_effort | Notes / Impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| v1 API endpoint surface is difficult to maintain (legacy sprawl) | High | 2026-03-23 | 2–4 weeks | Ongoing maintenance burden and slows new feature delivery; contributes to higher risk of regressions. Drives the need for deprecation and consolidation. |
| Missing/insufficient v1 → v2.x migration guide and upgrade playbook | High | 2026-03-23 | 3–5 days | Required to make deprecation (end of Q2 2026) feasible; without it, client churn/escalations risk increases. |
| No standardized deprecation mechanism (warnings/sunset headers/cutover plan) | High | 2026-03-23 | 1–2 weeks | Need consistent approach across endpoints (e.g., deprecation headers, documentation banners, staged disabling). |
| Insufficient version-level telemetry to track client migration progress (v1 vs v2.x traffic) | Medium | 2026-03-23 | 3–7 days | Without adoption metrics, it’s hard to validate readiness for v1 sunset and to prioritize client outreach. |
| Auth architecture gap for v2.3 (OAuth2 + JWT enforcement across services) | Medium | 2026-03-23 | 1–2 weeks | Requires consistent token validation, scopes/claims design, and integration points; risk of inconsistent enforcement if implemented ad-hoc. |
| Rate limiting implementation/operationalization (tier-based limits + monitoring) | Medium | 2026-03-23 | 4–8 days | Requires enforcement layer, configuration by plan, dashboards/alerts, and clear 429 behavior guidelines. |
| Frontend SDK lag risk (SDK not yet aligned with v2.3 auth changes) | Low | 2026-03-23 | 2–4 days | Not purely “debt” but a likely recurring gap if SDK release cadence isn’t aligned with API changes. |

## Next Actions (Owners from meeting)
- Mike: draft migration guide (target: end of week).
- Tom: staging environment for v2.3 testing.
- Lisa: frontend SDK updates for v2.3 auth.
- Sarah: stakeholder comms for v1 deprecation plan.
